data.narrative:
Digantara is a Series B space technology company that has raised $50M+ to date. The valuation analysis relies on a comparable universe of 4 same-stage private peers in Space & Aerospace (Aalyria, Stoke Space, Hubble Network, and Sift), supplemented by reference to 5 public sector comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two valuation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $50M raised as a proxy base and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yields $95M. Peer-median methodology is more informative: the four comparable companies trade at a median valuation of $700M, with a range spanning $274M (Sift) to $1.3B (Aalyria). This wider spread reflects heterogeneity in maturity and market position within the same stage cohort. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of disclosed revenue for the target.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $81M to $805M, with a median of $398M. The wide band reflects meaningful uncertainty about where within the peer distribution Digantara should trade. Stage-progression anchors the lower bound conservatively; peer median sits at the midpoint; the upper range reflects the highest-valued peer. Key variables include revenue trajectory, customer concentration, technology differentiation relative to peers, and capital efficiency. Resolution of these factors will be critical to positioning within this range.
